text: The Parable of the Shrewd Manager

segments.text: Jesus also said to His disciples,
There was a rich man whose manager was accused of wasting his possessions.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So he called him in to ask, ‘What is this I hear about you? Turn in an account of your management, for you cannot be manager any longer.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The manager said to himself, ‘What shall I do, now that my master is taking away my position? I am too weak to dig and too ashamed to beg.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I know what I will do, so that after my removal from management, people will welcome me into their homes.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And he called in each one of his master’s debtors. ‘How much do you owe my master?’ he asked the first.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text:
‘A hundred measures of olive oil,’ he answered.
‘Take your bill,’ said the manager. ‘Sit down quickly, and write fifty.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text:
Then he asked another, ‘And how much do you owe?’
‘A hundred measures of wheat,’ he replied.
‘Take your bill and write eighty,’ he told him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The master commended the dishonest manager because he had acted shrewdly. For the sons of this age are more shrewd in dealing with their own kind than are the sons of light.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you, use worldly wealth to make friends for yourselves, so that when it is gone, they will welcome you into eternal dwellings.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Whoever is faithful with very little will also be faithful with much, and whoever is dishonest with very little will also be dishonest with much.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So if you have not been faithful with worldly wealth, who will entrust you with true riches?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And if you have not been faithful with the belongings of another, who will give you belongings of your own?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: No servant can serve two masters. Either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve both God and money.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The Pharisees, who were lovers of money, heard all of this and were scoffing at Jesus.
So He said to them,
You are the ones who justify yourselves before men, but God knows your hearts. For what is prized among men is detestable before God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The Law and the Prophets were proclaimed until John. Since that time, the gospel of the kingdom of God is being preached, and everyone is forcing his way into it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for a single stroke of a pen to drop out of the Law.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Anyone who divorces his wife and marries another woman commits adultery, and he who marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Now there was a rich man dressed in purple and fine linen, who lived each day in joyous splendor.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And a beggar named Lazarus lay at his gate, covered with sores
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and longing to be fed with the crumbs that fell from the rich man’s table. Even the dogs came and licked his sores.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: One day the beggar died and was carried by the angels to Abraham’s side. And the rich man also died and was buried.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: In Hades, where he was in torment, he looked up and saw Abraham from afar, with Lazarus by his side.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So he cried out, ‘Father Abraham, have mercy on me and send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue. For I am in agony in this fire.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But Abraham answered, ‘Child, remember that during your lifetime you received your good things, while Lazarus received bad things. But now he is comforted here, while you are in agony.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And besides all this, a great chasm has been fixed between us and you, so that even those who wish cannot cross from here to you, nor can anyone cross from there to us.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: ‘Then I beg you, father,’ he said, ‘send Lazarus to my father’s house,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: for I have five brothers. Let him warn them, so that they will not also end up in this place of torment.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But Abraham replied, ‘They have Moses and the prophets; let your brothers listen to them.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: ‘No, father Abraham,’ he said, ‘but if someone is sent to them from the dead, they will repent.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then Abraham said to him, ‘If they do not listen to Moses and the prophets, they will not be persuaded even if someone rises from the dead.’
segments.speaker: Jesus
